title: ficus ardisioides subsp. camptoneura
intro: Ficus ardisioides subsp. camptoneura, also known as the Australian banyan, is a species of fig tree native to Australia and New Guinea. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and improve immune function, as well as its potential to treat digestive issues and fever. The herbal preparations of Ficus ardisioides subsp. camptoneura include infusions, tinctures, and poultices, which can be used externally or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
